t_ch: 遗迹里有不少怪物和失控的机兵，也有些不守规矩的同行会来抢你的成果，听说有人还看到了更可怕的东西……
t_en: There're lots of monsters and buggy automatons down there — not to mention some of my shady peers who won't hesitate to steal the fruits of our gains. And I've heard some talk about even more terrifying things...

t_ch: 不过也别太担心，风险高的地方收益也高，我相信你一定能找到法子解决！
t_en: But don't worry about it. Where there's a risk, there's a reward. I have faith in your problem-solving ability!

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: No more questions from me.

t_ch: 下次再说。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 行，但最好赶紧动手，别让同行抢先了。
t_en: Sure, but it's best to act soon and prevent others from getting the drop on us.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，这次的成果不错，辛苦你了！
t_en: Hey, not a bad haul this time. Good job!

t_ch: 你也辛苦。
t_en: You're not too bad yourself.

t_ch: 嗐，这对资深寻宝者来说算不了什么！
t_en: Ha, that's nothing to a senior treasure hunter.

t_ch: 你先休息一下，我接下来会开始挖掘通向更深处的通道。
t_en: Get some shut-eye. I'll start digging a passage down to the next level.

t_ch: 你好像不太辛苦。
t_en: You don't seem to be working that hard.

t_ch: 哎哟，这话也太伤人了，我可是拼了命地在找宝贝啊。
t_en: Dang, that's hurtful! I've been scrambling to find treasure over here.

t_ch: 你现在倒是能歇着了，我可闲不下来。我还得继续往下找更深的遗迹呢！
t_en: You're able to take a break now, but I can't. I have to keep digging to find even more hidden ruins!

t_ch: 想必你已经熟悉遗迹中的环境了，更深处的区域宝物也会更多，但肯定更危险。
t_en: You probably get how ruins work now. All the best ruins are down there in the depths — but it's also more dangerous the deeper you go.

t_ch: 做好准备吧，想要寻宝就随时来找我！
t_en: Go get yourself ready, and come find me anytime you want to go hunting for relics!

t_ch: 哈哈，我果然没看错人。有你陪着，咱俩的探宝之旅真是无往不利！
t_en: Haha, so I was right after all. Together, our relic-hunting journey is gonna take on the world!

t_ch: 对了，之前探索过的遗迹可以继续「深入探索」，不过路径上想必会更加危险…就看你敢不敢了。
t_en: Oh yeah — you can continue to explore the ruin depths at any ruins that you've already been through, but things will become even more dangerous... It's all up to how brave you are.

t_ch: 果然越深处就越危险啊…还好有你，不然这些古代遗物就要变成咱的陪葬品了……
t_en: It's getting more and more dangerous the deeper we go... Lucky you're here, otherwise these relics would end up being our graves...

t_ch: 但和我之前设想的一样，越往深处走，发掘出来的古代遗物也就越值钱。现在可不是打退堂鼓的时候，准备去更深处的遗迹吧！
t_en: As predicted, we're seeing more valuable relics the deeper we go. Now's no time to give up. Let's drill on down to the depths!

t_ch: 哎，真不容易，没想到能一路挖到这么深的地方。
t_en: Whew, that took it out of me. I never imagined we'd end up so deep in one go.

t_ch: 以我的经验，再往下就是遗迹的最深处了…听说有人在那边遇到过可怕的机械巨人，和之前沿路碰见的杂兵完全不一样，你可一定得保护好我啊！
t_en: Based on my experience, the next one down is the bottom floor of the ruins... Somebody once said they saw a terrifying giant robot down there, completely different from any pawns that we met before. You have to protect me!

t_ch: 呼——不愧是你，居然能让咱俩全身而退！
t_en: Huh — you really ARE good. We managed to both get out of there in one piece!

t_ch: 这次差不多把遗迹翻了个底朝天，赚了这么多，估计其他寻宝者的后槽牙都要咬碎了！
t_en: This time we really looted every last scrap from that ruin, and we made a nice score. Those other relic hunters are gonna be steaming mad!

t_ch: 感觉赚得也不多啊…
t_en: Doesn't seem like we'll be earning much from this...

t_ch: 呃…出钱的人可是那位「深蓝帅哥」，那哥们儿是我认识最靠谱的购货商了，他不可能坑咱们！
t_en: Ahem... The buyer is that guy called Tall, Blue, and Handsome. He's the most reliable trader I know, and he'd never swindle us!

t_ch: 他说最近出土的古代遗物太多了，物多则贱，你就知足吧！
t_en: He said there've been a lot of relics dug up recently and you know how that tanks the market. Be happy with your lot!

t_ch: 对了，不久之后我就要去其他地方寻宝了…在离开前，你要是想再进到遗迹里搜刮…啊不是，探索几趟，我也奉陪。
t_en: Oh yeah, I'm going off treasure-hunting somewhere else soon... Before I go, if you wanna go back in to hunt for relics one last time — ahh, I mean as many times you like — well, I'll be there!

t_ch: 说不定咱们之前还遗漏了什么好宝贝呢…你说是吧？
t_en: Perhaps there's something juicy that we missed before... What say you?

t_ch: 是你啊，准备好去古代遗迹寻宝了吗？
t_en: Ah, it's you again. Ready to go treasure hunting down in the ancient ruins?

t_ch: 进入古代遗迹。
t_en: Enter the ancient ruins.

t_ch: 好嘞，我们出发吧！
t_en: Okay, let's get going!

t_ch: 我想问关于古代遗迹的事。
t_en: I've got questions about the ancient ruins.

t_ch: 问吧，我很乐意为伙伴排忧解难！
t_en: Ask away. I'd be happy to help set you at ease, partner!

t_ch: 这个古代遗迹是怎么来的？
t_en: How did the ancient ruins come about?

t_ch: 都叫古代遗迹了，那当然是古代人造的呗。
t_en: We call it the ancient ruins because some ancient people built them.

t_ch: 你应该也听说过，在寒潮降临之前，天上来的怪物入侵了这里，这应该是那时古人留下的避难所。
t_en: You probably heard about how monsters from the sky invaded this place before the Eternal Freeze. This should be a kind of shelter for people back then.

t_ch: 我们能找到什么古代遗物？
t_en: What ancient relics could we find there?

t_ch: 说不准，古代遗物千奇百怪，很多东西我也叫不出名字。
t_en: I'm not sure. There're lots of varied and strange relics there, and I don't even know what most of these are called.

t_ch: 但不管找到啥玩意儿，能卖个好价钱就行了，你说是吧？
t_en: But no matter what we find, what's important is that we can sell it for top money — right?

t_ch: 古代遗迹里危险吗？
t_en: Is it dangerous in the ancient ruins?

t_ch: 这…危不危险你不都感受到了吗？
t_en: Can't... Can't you tell already?

t_ch: 还好有你，不然还真就要栽在地下了…看吧，我就说你一定能找到法子解决！
t_en: Lucky I have you, otherwise I'd no doubt be six feet under... See, I told you I have faith in your problem-solving ability!

t_ch: 没有什么想问的了。
t_en: No more questions from me.

t_ch: 关于结束探宝的感想。
t_en: Thoughts after the treasure hunting is over.

t_ch: 坦白讲，这次真是我寻宝经历里最刺激的一次！以前遇到怪物只能开溜，这次却能一路闯到底……
t_en: To be completely honest, this has been the most thrilling treasure hunt in all my years! Previously I'd have to run away whenever I run into a monster, but this time we made it to the very bottom...

t_ch: 你真是合格的寻宝者护卫，今后有机会再合作啊！
t_en: You really are a first-rate relic-hunter bodyguard! Let's team up again if we ever get the chance!

t_ch: 下次再说。
t_en: Maybe next time.

t_ch: 行，反正已经探到最深处了，想再进去就随时找我吧！
t_en: Okay, in any case, we've already reached the bottom. So just hit me up if you want to go down again!

t_ch: 旁听
t_en: Listen

t_ch: 我们去深处寻宝吧！
t_en: Let's go hunt for relics down in the depths!

t_ch: 下面很危险……
t_en: It's dangerous down there...

t_ch: 上层的东西都被挖空了，我们去下面看看吧！
t_en: Everything on the surface has all been carved out. Let's take a look further down!

t_ch: 别别别，你没听说底下有怪物吗？
t_en: No no no, haven't you heard about the monsters down there?

t_ch: 怕什么？我可是搏击俱乐部排行榜的三百强选手！
t_en: So what? I'm top <unbreak>300</unbreak> on the Fight Club leaderboard!

t_ch: 排行榜一共才多少人…就算能打赢，先下去不就是给后面的人开路嘛，多亏啊！
t_en: There're probably only <unbreak>300</unbreak> fighters there... And even if we go down and win, we're just doing all the work for the next person coming along. That's not worth it!

t_ch: 啧，你说的也有道理……
t_en: Tsk, good point...

t_ch: 让我去寻宝！
t_en: I wanna go hunt relics!

t_ch: 冷静，冷静……
t_en: Calm down, calm down...

t_ch: 可恶，那个叫阿克赛的家伙赚了那么多，我也要下去！
t_en: Darn it, that guy Aksay got so much loot... I wanna go down right now!

t_ch: 别别别，宝贝都被他挖走了，还下去犯这个险干吗？
t_en: No no no, he's cleared out all the treasure. Why take that risk and go down there?

t_ch: 要不是你拦着我，挖出宝贝的就是我！
t_en: I'd have dug those treasures out already if you hadn't stopped me!

t_ch: 要不是我拦着你，估计你就得被他挖出来了……
t_en: You'd be dug out alright if I hadn't stopped you...

t_ch: 这个古代遗物是干什么的？
t_en: What is this relic used for?

t_ch: 让我来看看……
t_en: Let me see...

t_ch: 大哥，刚刚挖出来个奇怪的玩意，要不要拿给巴列维看看？
t_en: Bro, I just dug up this weird thing. Wanna go show it to Balaway?

t_ch: 浪费那个钱干啥？我来看看就行！这个东西有扳机，还能喷热风……
t_en: And waste our money on that guy? I can handle this just fine! This thing... has a trigger, and it can spray out hot air...

t_ch: 大哥，你真认识吗？
t_en: Bro, are you being serious?

t_ch: 当然，这看上去就是个武器，应该叫…「热风炮」！古代的武器可不得了，估计比银鬃铁卫的大炮还狠！
t_en: Of course! Why, it looks like a weapon... Probably a Hot-Air Pistol! These ancient weapons are incredible. I bet it's way more vicious than a Silvermane cannon!

t_ch: 真的吗？咱们拿着它去遗迹深处闯闯吧！
t_en: Really? Let's take it down for a test run in the lower depths!

t_ch: 这东西原来不值钱啊……
t_en: So it's not worth anything...

t_ch: 哎哟，差点把命丢了……
t_en: Yikes, I almost died there...

t_ch: 大哥，你没事吧？我给巴列维看了，他说这东西可能是用来风干头发的……
t_en: Bro, are you okay? I took it to Balaway for a look, and he reckoned that it might just be used to dry ancient people's hair...

t_ch: 唉，怪不得。对着怪物吹了半天，一点威力都没有，差点把命丢了！
t_en: Ah, so that's why... I blew it at a monster for quite a while but it didn't do a thing, and I almost lost my life!

t_ch: 是啊，让这玩意害惨了！巴列维说他出五十冬城盾回收，要不卖了吧？
t_en: Yeah, curse this darn thing! Balaway offered me <unbreak>50</unbreak> shield to recycle it. Should we sell it to him?

t_ch: 卖什么卖，留着吧，以后洗完头还能用。
t_en: Sell it? No way. Let's keep it for the next time we wash our hair.

t_ch: 叫我来做什么？
t_en: Is there anything I can do?

t_ch: 哈，来得倒挺快。我做了个小插件，用它来存档一些有用的数据，{NICKNAME}，你帮我测下存档功能就行，我会给你报酬的。为了考虑你，这个插件也做得很好玩，感激我吧。
t_en: You're quick. Well, I've made a small plugin to save important data. {NICKNAME}, assist me in testing its file-saving function, and I'll pay for your service. You should appreciate that I make the plugin fun for you.

t_ch: 这个插件用来干嘛的？
t_en: What does this plugin do?

t_ch: 这还要我解释？你可以用它存档你已有的祝福。不过嘛，测试好功能后，我会用它去收集你的脑内神经电波，封存你脑中的部分数据。
t_en: Do I have to explain that? You can use it to store your Blessing. Just so you know, I'll use it to collect your brainwave and seal off some data in your brain after the test.

t_ch: 为什么记录我的神经电波？
t_en: Why do you need my brainwave?

t_ch: 你的脑内推演生成了许多莫名其妙的星球，我们想从中挑选点有用的东西；换句话来说，你脑子里的废料太多了！
t_en: You have imagined a lot of bizarre planets in your head, and we'd like to filter out some useful info. In other words, you've got too much nonsense in your brain!

t_ch: 所以，阮•梅想做点实验，先弄点数据保存在插件里，方便我们和螺丝咕姆做下一次大型改进！我不想剧透，反正你期待着就是了。
t_en: So, Ruan Mei plans to run an experiment. We'll store some data in the plugin for the next large-scale update that we and Screwllum deliver! I don't want to spoil the surprise. Anyway, just look forward to it.

t_ch: 存了些什么数据？
t_en: What kind of data is stored?

t_ch: 祝福，你见过的人，和一些战斗啰。当然，这些都是给你用来练手的，用来推演星神的重要数据我还在下载，不过不着急。
t_en: Blessings, people you've met, and some battles. Of course, these are to help you get the hang of things. I'm still downloading the important data used to deduce the Aeon. There's no rush.

t_ch: 我没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any other questions.

t_ch: 嗯，祝你玩得愉快啰。
t_en: Have a good time.

t_ch: 听说你有求于我…
t_en: I heard you're looking for me...

t_ch: 嗐，您瞧在下这脑袋，尽想着评书了，怎么把这茬儿给忘了。想起来了，在下确实是有求于您。
t_en: Ah, would you look at this brain of mine? Lost in my storytelling world again, how could I just forget about that? Which reminds me — I have a favor to ask you.

t_ch: 这段时日咱们仙舟发生了不少事，又是建木生发，又是药王秘传兴风作浪…新闻上都说，能平息这些灾难，您{NICKNAME}功不可没。
t_en: There's been a lot going on aboard the Xianzhou recently, with the Ambrosial Arbor's new growth and those Disciples of Sanctus Medicus causing a ruckus... And all the news feeds report that you've played no small part, {NICKNAME}, in keeping the peace.

t_ch: 但市面上暂时还没有人以此为题材产出过什么文艺作品，在下这不就想着能捷足先登，狠狠赚一点票房吗。
t_en: But there's no literature out there on the market right now really capitalizing on this. I'm just thinking, why not get ahead of the curve and make a bit of a killing at the box office, hmm?

t_ch: 所以在下才请您过来，讲讲您的英雄故事。在下要是拿到了您这位当事人的第一手材料，那必然能创作出一折精彩的评书啊！
t_en: And so, I humbly invite you to tell me of your heroic exploits. I'll definitely spin out a brilliant story if I could just get my hands on those first-hand accounts of yours!

t_ch: 就为了这点小事叫我过来啊…
t_en: You summoned me for this triviality?

t_ch: 把剧情给你讲一遍就行了呗？
t_en: So, you want a recap of what happened?

t_ch: 好嘞！那就麻烦您了！
t_en: Wonderful! If you could be so kind!

t_ch: 西衍先生拿出手机，准备记录下你讲的故事。
t_en: Mr. Xiyan takes out his phone and prepares to record your tales.

t_ch: 「这个男人叫做老杨，这个女人叫做小七…」你学着网上「三分钟看完托蝶幻境」的方式，讲起了自己的罗浮冒险记。
t_en: "In a world where memes and cringe collide, one Trailblazer must rise to the challenge! It all started with this cute girl known as Little Miss March... and then some old wise man called Grandpa Yang..." You rattle off your journey so far aboard the Luofu in a deep dramatic voice, adopting the style of the hit web drama "3-Minute Butterfly Immersia."

t_ch: 从初到仙舟，到血战幻胧…你尽可能将故事讲得跌宕起伏，可西衍先生的注意力却愈发涣散。
t_en: From your arrival on the Xianzhou to your bloody battle with Phantylia, you do your best to cover all the ups and downs and twists and turns of your story... But Mr. Xiyan's attention span seems to be waning.

t_ch: 讲到「云霞紫府会战」这一节时，他甚至将手机横了过来——大概是偷偷玩起了帝垣琼玉。
t_en: He even turned his phone sideways during "The Battle of Evemist Mansion" — no doubt to sneak in a couple of hands of Celestial Jade.

t_ch: 「这个{M#男人}{F#女人}自号『银河球棒侠』，此刻{F#她}{M#他}正与祸乱仙舟的药王秘传魁首对峙…而这，将是一场事关生死的较量！」
t_en: "This {M#man}{F#woman} who called {M#him}{F#her}self "Galactic Baseballer" was locked in a heated battle against the master primus of the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus... And for the Xianzhou, this was a battle for life and death!"

t_ch: 你讲完了，西衍先生也神色一喜——这老小子和牌了。
t_en: You finally finish telling your story, and Mr. Xiyan looks pretty pleased, too — this old fox has apparently played a winning hand.

t_ch: 胡了？不，我是说，结束了？别这样看着我，在下确实一直在认真听您讲故事。
t_en: Good game... Ahem, I mean, good story! You are done there with the story, right? Don't look at me like that. I've been listening attentively the whole time.

t_ch: 但是吧，您的故事虽然…不能算无趣吧，却有逻辑复杂、情节平淡、看开头知结尾等等诸多问题在内……
t_en: Your story, however, has some issues: Even though the plot is not that boring, it's written in a way that is overstretched, underwhelming, and predictable. I can see where it's going from a mile away...

t_ch: 贵在真实啊！
t_en: But that's how it happened! It's a true story!

t_ch: 在下也说句真话您别不爱听：真实的故事谁爱看啊！想看真实的故事，直接去看新闻就好了啊！
t_en: Let me tell you the truth, hard as it is to hear: Who cares about the truth!? If you want the truth, just go and watch the news instead!

t_ch: 把钱结了，咱们还是朋友。
t_en: Whatever~ Just pay me the money for my story, and we can still be on friendly terms.

t_ch: 哎呀您别生气呀，在下不是这个意思……
t_en: Come on. Don't be like that. I didn't mean it like that...

t_ch: 在下只是觉得，想要让您的传奇故事在罗浮乃至整个仙舟长久的流传下去，需要适当做一些戏剧化的改编。
t_en: I just feel that if you want your legend to pass far and wide throughout the Luofu — or even all of Xianzhou, — then we'll need to take some creative liberties.

t_ch: 而且听您聊战斗…嗯，怎么说呢…嗯…好朴实啊！你一来我一回的，有点单调。
t_en: And hearing you talk about combat... *sigh* How should I put it... Hmm... It's really stale! You hit me, then I hit you — it's a bit monotonous.

t_ch: 您知道仙舟上流行的幻戏吧？呃，搁公司那头也许该叫交互式幻境体验。喝，那里面的战斗，可刺激多了。什么飞檐走壁、时空断裂，要什么有什么……
t_en: You know about the Immersia genre we have here on the Xianzhou, right? Or perhaps it should be called an interactive fantasy experience, despite what IPC thinks. Well, those fights are exhilarating. Leaping onto roofs and over walls, splitting open time and space, anything goes...

t_ch: 总而言之啊，我觉得这个故事，缺乏吸引观众的刺激要素！
t_en: Overall, I think this story is lacking a few exciting elements that we need to draw in the audience!

t_ch: 但它的商业潜力无限，怎么办呢…这样吧…请您稍等一下，在下找个东西给您。
t_en: But its commercial potential is unlimited. What should I do... Please hold on one second. I'm just fetching something for you.

t_ch: 这个东西，干咱们这行的称之为「主角模板」。据说是某位幻戏社的编剧发明的编剧工业化神器。
t_en: This thing is what we in the industry call a Protagonist Model. Legend has it that some Immersia troupe has invented this magical tool for industrial-scale screenwriting.

t_ch: 虽说一千个无名客心里会有一千个阿基维利的形象，但也许您听说过…人类一直传诵的经典传说和故事的主角们拢共就那么几十种。
t_en: And even though Akivili takes on thousands of different forms within thousands of different Nameless, you may have heard that... Actually, there're only a dozen or so archetypal protagonists recycled through these classic legends and tales.

t_ch: 可见，吸引人的故事是有规律可循的。而这东西便是就此应运而生。
t_en: This shows that there are rules to follow when telling an alluring story. And that's how this thing rose to meet the occasion.

t_ch: 您瞅瞅，只要给主角的英雄之旅中安排好各种奇遇，再让主角在这个过程中获得各种神奇的能力和宝物…最后华丽地战胜强敌！一个精彩的故事就这么成了！
t_en: Take a gander. All you have to do is set up a diverse range of encounters for a protagonist along a Hero's Journey, and let the protagonist gain various mystical abilities and treasures... Before magnificently defeating a powerful foe! That's how you make a wonderful story!

t_ch: 解释起来还是有些苍白，您直接拿着看看吧。
t_en: My explanation alone can't do it justice. You'd better take a look yourself.

t_ch: 总而言之，「主角模板」就是这样一个简单好用的神器…只要照它的套路指引，就可以创作出令听众血脉偾张、欲罢不能的评书。
t_en: In short, the Protagonist Model is like a simple magic wand... So long as you follow its patterns, you can easily craft pulse-racing heart-pumping stories for your audience.

t_ch: 这样吧，咱们一起去一趟您战斗过的地方——丹鼎司。故地重游，一定可以激发出咱们的灵感，将这个「主角模板」充实起来。
t_en: How about this — you and I hit up a site of your former battles: The Alchemy Commission. Revisiting old haunts will definitely get the creative juices flowing. Let's flesh out this Protagonist Model.

t_ch: 那我好人做到底吧…

t_en: Don't say that! I'll make it worth your while~ You can refuse me, but you won't refuse money, will you?

t_ch: 走吧，咱该去丹鼎司了～
t_en: Alright! Let's push on then to the Alchemy Commission~

t_ch: 如果在下没记错的话，您这场冒险的大决战，就是从云霞紫府这边开始的吧？
t_en: If I was not mistaken, the decisive battle in your adventure began right over here at the Evemist Mansion, correct?

t_ch: 没错。
t_en: Correct.

t_ch: 怎么直接就聊决战了？
t_en: Why are we going straight to this decisive battle?

t_ch: 哎！您有所不知，观众时间宝贵，耐心是越发少了，大家恨不得一分钟看完一部戏。故事推进太慢可是创作大忌。
t_en: Ah, you don't understand. You see, the audience's time is precious, and their patience ever thinning. People want it all to be over in a minute, a whole show. Slow-paced storytelling is a big no-no.

t_ch: 连咱们说书的都得随客人的习惯来，开头一段暖场，啪地一下就直接切入最精彩的段落。
t_en: Even we storytellers have to take our cues from the audience. Get them warmed up with an icebreaker, then with a snap, we'll cut straight to the most exciting part.

t_ch: 按您的说法，您在这儿和同伴一起挑战了药王秘传的魁首。这场大战中可有遇到什么艰难波折？又或者，她施了什么妖法诡计，教各位求生不得求死不能？
t_en: You said that this was where you and your companions fought against the master primus of the Sanctus Medicus. Was there any conflict that you encountered here? Or perhaps, did she stoop to any dastardly tricks to really put you in between a rock and a hard place?

t_en: H—how is that possible? How can a great battle be exciting without any swerves or setbacks?

t_en: Listen up — "The Legion was in cahoots with the master primus of the Sanctus Medicus. Using their arcane powers, they activated elixir cauldrons to spew forth venomous vapors as they tried to take out {NICKNAME} and the other munificent heroes in one fell swoop!"

t_en: "The situation grew so grave that {NICKNAME} and {F#her}{M#his} companions concocted a genius plan..."

t_ch: 「进退维谷之际，忽然醒觉此处乃是丹鼎司，本是炼就灵丹妙药的福地！」
t_en: "Stuck between a rock and a hard place, the realization suddenly dawned that this was still the Alchemy Commission: A paradise known for forging the elixir of life!"

t_ch: 「药王秘传既得烬灭祸祖的恩赐，那我等亦可从丹鼎司中求取仙丹，洗髓伐毛，脱胎换骨，凭空增加个数百甲子的功力！」
t_en: "The Disciples of Sanctus Medicus have received their mandate from the Ruin Author. We though can strive for a miracle pill from the Alchemy Commission, turning over a new leaf to be born anew through generations of cultivated power!"

t_en: A glorious treasure: The elixir of life. Take it for a shortcut past centuries of martial art practice and cultivation. Then suddenly, pow! We're smashing enemies left and right. What's not to love about that?

t_en: Don't say that! The first Cauldron Master was thinking ahead about any misuse that might harm the Xianzhou, you see... Or is that a bit far-fetched? No problem. Don't even worry about it. This is just a first script, a first script I tell you. We'll just fix it in post.

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}知道，能感受到这温柔的气息，是因为这位勇者已经成功渡过了最危险的试炼…如今呈现在{NICKNAME}面前的，乃是初代司舵的祝福。」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} knew, this warm sensation meant that the most dangerous part of the trial had already passed... What now greeted {NICKNAME} was the first Helm Master's blessing."

t_ch: 「起飞吧！{NICKNAME}！迎接您最后的试炼吧！」
t_en: "Take off, {NICKNAME}! Welcome to this, your final trial!"

t_ch: 「有了前两处幻境的经验…」
t_en: "Drawing on experience from the previous two fantasias..."

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}必能将这些智慧活用此处，找到司鼎秘藏之物。」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} was determined to put this knowledge to use and find the Cauldron Master's secret treasure."

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}又一次完成了试炼，却发觉自己陷入了一片漫无边际的白雾之中。当白雾散尽，{NICKNAME}看到一棵巨树，四周垂荡着无数气生根。」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} completed yet another trial, only to find {F#her}{M#him}self trapped in a vast white fog. Once it cleared, {NICKNAME} saw a huge tree with floating roots."

t_ch: 「此处分明没有半点微风，那些气生根却在空中摇摆着，沿着它们摇摆的轨迹，空气被劈开，雷光闪烁。」
t_en: "Despite no obvious breeze, the floating roots swayed in the air, cleaving the very air in twain with flashes of lighting paired to each swing."

t_ch: 「那正是丹鼎司所藏最为珍贵的药材——『风雷天根』。{NICKNAME}衔下长势最好的气生根，离开了初代司鼎的幻境。」
t_en: "This then was the most precious medicinal herb hidden at the Alchemy Commission — the Whirlbolt Pneumatophore. {NICKNAME} harvested the best of the floating roots, and left this fantasia of the first Cauldron Master."

t_ch: 没给我电出毛病来？
t_en: "Whirlbolt"... Wouldn't that shock me?

t_ch: 怎么会呢？「风雷天根」产生的电流，乃是疏通经脉、按摩肌肉、软化血管的不二之法，并没有什么危险的。
t_en: How could it? The Whirlbolt Pneumatophore's electricity merely unblocks the meridians as it massages and stimulates blood flow. It's a great remedy and not at all dangerous.

t_ch: 听着不像机巧鸟该碰的玩意儿…
t_en: That doesn't sound like something a cycrane should touch...

t_ch: 不不不，那不是机巧鸟，那是您至真至纯、不受引力束缚的心灵，在初代司鼎的幻境中呈现出的结果。可不能按照机巧鸟的逻辑来理解呀。
t_en: No no no, that's not actually a cycrane. It's the result of your true, pure, unfettered soul projected into the first Cauldron Master's fantasia. You can't really think of it in terms of a normal cycrane.

t_ch: 你胡编乱造也得有个度…
t_en: There is a limit to suspension of disbelief, you know...

t_ch: 观众不就好这口吗？这年头娱乐行业竞争这么激烈，咱们这些靠一张嘴吹的，凭什么和天花乱坠的幻戏抢夺用户啊？答案只有一个，离谱！
t_en: It's what the audience wants, though. Competition in entertainment circles is so fierce these days. How are we storytellers supposed to compete with all the razzle-dazzle and hype of something like immersia? There's only one answer: Manufactured outrage!

t_ch: 这又是现编的吗？
t_en: You're just making it all up as you go, aren't you?

t_ch: 咱说书先生，不就靠这个来赚钱糊口吗？
t_en: Isn't that how we storytellers earn our crust, day in day out?

t_ch: 这年头娱乐行业竞争这么激烈，咱们这些靠一张嘴吹的，凭什么和天花乱坠的幻戏抢夺用户啊？答案只有一个，离谱！
t_en: Competition in entertainment circles is so fierce these days. How are we storytellers supposed to compete with all the razzle-dazzle and hype of something like immersia? There's only one answer: Manufactured outrage!

t_ch: 幻戏做不到的我能说，幻戏不敢编的我能说。反正吹牛不上税，说出来的话就像一阵风……
t_en: I can say all the stuff they can't do in immersia. And I can say what they don't dare say in immersia. Anyway, there's no tax on bragging, and words have a habit of disappearing like the wind...

t_ch: 咳咳，言归正传……
t_en: Ahem, back to the topic at hand...

t_ch: 「至此，{NICKNAME}已经拿到了炼制『九转升天丹』所需要的全部三味药材…接下来，只要在丹炉中将三味药材九转烧炼，便可获得这脱胎换骨的神丹了！」
t_en: "Previously, our hero {NICKNAME} acquired all three medicinal herbs required to concoct the Elixir of Dragon Oil... All that remained was to cast the ingredients in the elixir crucible and this miracle elixir would be {F#hers}{M#his}!"

t_ch: 「至此，{NICKNAME}已经拿到了炼制『九转升天丹』所需要的全部三味药材…接下来，只要在丹炉中将三味药材九转烧炼，便可获得这脱胎换骨的神丹了！」
t_en: "Previously, our hero {NICKNAME} acquired all three medicinal herbs required to concoct the Elixir of Dragon Oil... All that remained was to cast the ingredients in the elixir crucible and this miracle elixir would be {F#hers}{M#his}!"

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}将三味药材依次投进了丹炉中。先是『冰火玄莲』，它身上蕴含的至温至寒，在丹炉中调和为一，迸发出无穷的自然伟力。」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} placed the three medicinal herbs into the crucible, one by one. First came the Icefire Lotus: Its fiery heat and blasting cold blended together, bursting out infinite natural force."

t_ch: 「接着是『生灭灵芝』，它有无相生，将使冰与火的矛盾得以长久共存。」
t_en: "Next, came the Life-Death Polypore: Its quantum impermanence stabilizes the paradox inherent in the lotus."

t_ch: 「最后，{NICKNAME}将『风雷天根』投入丹炉中，霎时间电闪雷鸣，丹炉那方寸洞天中竟下起了一场雷雨！」
t_en: "Finally, {NICKNAME} tossed the Whirlbolt Pneumatophore into the crucible. With a flash of thunder and lightning, out of the blue an entire thunderstorm formed inside one square inch of the crucible!"

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}鼓起勇气，将手探入雷雨之中。片刻之后，一颗散发着绚烂色彩的丹药便被{NICKNAME}取了出来。」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} plucked up the courage to reach a hand into the storm. After a while, {NICKNAME}'s hand pulled out a sparkling medicinal pellet."

t_ch: 「那便是可令人永登仙道…」呃，不对，这个词不能乱说…「总之就是脱胎换骨、神功大成的灵丹妙药，『九转升天丹』！」
t_en: "And that, my friend, is what leads a mortal onto the path of IMMORTALITY..." Uh oh! No no no no... I shouldn't have used that word on the Xianzhou... "Anyways... It's basically a miracle drug that makes you be born anew and achieve transcendence. Behold, the Elixir of Dragon Oil!"

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}吞下神丹，顿时觉得身体轻盈仿若云朵，肌肉充盈恰似牡牛，顷刻间可飞驰三千里，抬手便能举起万斤鼎！」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} swallowed the miracle elixir, and instantly felt as light as a feather but as strong as an ox. Our hero could leap thirty thousand miles in a second, or lift up ten thousand tons!"

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}知道，现在是时候去找那些祸乱仙舟、可悲可憎的药王秘传算算账了！」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} knew, now was the time to settle the score with those pathetic, detestable Disciples of Sanctus Medicus wreaking havoc on the Xianzhou."

t_ch: 怎么听着感觉我就是药王秘传…
t_en: The way you tell it makes me sound like I'm a Disciple of Sanctus Medicus...

t_ch: 这里面到底谁是反派啊…
t_en: Are we... the baddies?

t_ch: 嗐，瞧您这话说的…力量没有善恶，人才有。厨刀是在厨师手里，还是在凶徒手里，那肯定是不一样的。
t_en: Ha, listen to yourself... Power in and of itself knows no good or bad. Only people do. Is a kitchen knife evil when it's in the hands of a chef or when it's in the hands of a murderer?

t_ch: 「闲话少叙！请列位看官倾耳听！下一段便是重头戏——『丹鼎司浩劫终难逃，无名客血战寿瘟妖』！」
t_en: "Less idle chit-chat! Please ladies and gentlemen, esteemed guests: Lend me your ears! Next up we have the main feature: 'Calamity Ensnares Commission of Alchemy, Nameless Battles Author of Plagues!'"

t_ch: 哎呀，这样一来，这折子戏就算是完成了……
t_en: Ah, if we put it like that, then this act will be complete...

t_ch: 这就完成了？
t_en: That's what you call complete?

t_ch: 「血战」让你给吃了？
t_en: What happened to the fight against the Plagues Author demon?

t_ch: 嗐，这您有所不知，在咱们编剧行业，最难写的莫过于主角的成长…主角的成长有了着落，要多来几场血战还不简单！
t_en: Ah, you don't understand. The most difficult thing for us screenwriters is the protagonist's development... Once the protagonist's character is settled, it's gonna be so easy to just write in as many battles as you want!

t_ch: 这样，这儿不是落笔写稿的好地方…您先跟我回一趟星槎海，待我把今天的故事和灵感整理一下，咱们来聊聊战斗的戏码。
t_en: Look, this isn't exactly the best writing place for us... Come back to the Starskiff Haven with me, and after I process all of today's inspiration, we can chat more about combat details.

t_ch: 你可瞧见那些灵气氤氲、闪闪发亮的光点？
t_en: Can you see those glowing, sparkly lights up ahead?

t_ch: 这边是你进入幻境的目的，将这些丹药统统收入囊中！
t_en: This is the point of going into the fantasia — to gather up all these medicinal pellets!

t_ch: 你获得了「冰火玄莲」，可喜可贺。
t_en: You got an Icefire Lotus. Well done.

t_ch: 小心，别被怪物赶上喽！
t_en: Careful, don't get caught by monsters!

t_ch: 这边是你进入幻境的目的，将这些丹药统统收入囊中！
t_en: This is the point of going into the fantasia — to gather up all these medicinal pellets!

t_ch: 你获得了「生灭灵芝」，可喜可贺。
t_en: You got a Life-Death Polypore. Well done.

t_ch: 你获得了「风雷天根」，可喜可贺。
t_en: You got a Whirlbolt Pneumatophore. Well done.

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}气运丹田，破除了幻境中的禁制……」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} concentrated all {F#her}{M#his} qi to {F#her}{M#his} Core Radix, thus breaking the containment mechanism in the fantasia..."

t_ch: 「随着所有禁制都被{NICKNAME}破除，初代司鼎的秘宝也终于显露了出来……」
t_en: "As all the containment mechanisms were gradually lifted by {NICKNAME}, the secret treasure of the first Cauldron Master was revealed..."

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}定睛观瞧——原来那里正好有一物，可用来分散恶兽的注意！」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} took a closer look — and there just so happened to be something useful for distracting the beast!"

t_ch: 是不是该写评书第二回了…
t_en: Is it time to write Act II...

t_ch: 您瞧瞧，一忙就顾不上那许多了。差点又忘了和您约好的事。
t_en: You see, you can't take care of much when you're busy. I almost forgot we agreed to meet.

t_ch: 这段时间在下也没闲着，一直在构思接下来的情节。但确实有个问题，在下一直没找到解决方案……
t_en: I've been keeping myself busy, coming up with the rest of the plot. But we may have a problem to which I haven't been able to find an answer yet...

t_ch: 按您原本的冒险故事…剿灭了那些药王秘传，居然立刻就和绝灭大君照面决战了。这故事进展展开的也太快了吧？
t_en: Based on your original story... After taking out the Disciples of Sanctus Medicus, you almost immediately fought your decisive battle against the Lord Ravager Phantylia. Don't you think that's pushing the story along too fast?

t_ch: 真要按照这个结构写个评书出来，一天就能讲完了，根本轮不到我拍一拍醒木，说「且听下回分解」，然后骗他们第二天再来买票啊。
t_en: If that's all I had, why, I'd finish telling this story in a single session. I wouldn't get the chance to set up one of my cliffhangers goading everyone into buying tickets for tomorrow. "Stay tuned for next time with Mr. Xiyan..."

t_ch: 而且啊，按这个做法，剧情里的「主角模板」也不够完善啊。所以在下有个思路，不知道可不可行，想和您商量一下……
t_en: And I tell you what, the Protagonist Model isn't perfect enough if we do the plot this way. So, I've come up with an idea. I'm not sure what you think, so I'd like to run it by you...

t_ch: 无名客不止您一个，那几位英雄也都在这段冒险中贡献了自己的力量。是否有可能，把别人的事迹移花接木到您身上？
t_en: Nameless, you're not the only one, right? Those other heroes have also thrown themselves into this adventure. Is it possible to perhaps fudge things a little so that YOU will be the one doing those deeds?

t_ch: 那我不成欺世盗名了？
t_en: Wouldn't that make me a fraud!?

t_ch: 对喽，就是这么回事！呃，不对……
t_en: Yeah, but... I mean, uh, no...?

t_ch: 在下的意思是，能不能把「无名客」的事迹汇聚到同一个典型人物上？
t_en: What I mean is, could some of the Nameless' heroic deeds be pulled together and attributed to a single archetypal character?

t_ch: 这样塑造一个典型人物，观众老爷们也更容易情感代入，是这个道理吧？
t_en: Creating archetypes like this makes it easier for our audience to become emotionally involved. How about it?

t_ch: 也好，毕竟我是主角。
t_en: That's okay, I guess. After all, I am the protagonist.

t_ch: 您有这觉悟，我老高兴了。
t_en: I'm so happy to hear you say that.

t_ch: 那在这次冒险过程里，有哪位同伴的故事适合加入这个故事呢？
t_en: So then, are there any companions whose exploits are suitable for us to add to our story?

t_ch: 有个叫丹恒的伙伴…
t_en: There's one called Dan Heng...

t_ch: 还有个同伴也来到了仙舟…
t_en: There's this other companion who also came to Xianzhou...

t_ch: 你向西衍先生讲起了丹恒一路追赶你们，以及他在鳞渊境中遭遇的冒险。
t_en: You fill in Mr. Xiyan about Dan Heng chasing you all the way, and the adventures he has encountered at Scalegorge Waterscape.

t_ch: 你这龙尊的故事倒是有意思，只是颇有些棘手。枝蔓横生，条缕纷乱。和我自己编的那些云上五骁的传奇可差远了。
t_en: This high elder story of yours is interesting, but a little tricky to pull off. It branches off in all directions with some red herrings. It's way different from the masterpieces I've personally written about the High-Cloud Quintet.

t_ch: 所以，需要大刀阔斧地改编！除了头尾，统统砍掉，也许还能写一个好故事。
t_en: Therefore, a drastic adaptation is required! We'll cut off everything apart from the beginning and the end. Maybe we can fix this story after all.

t_ch: 在下有点眉目了…走吧，咱们亲自去一趟鳞渊境，也许在那个龙尊造像下面，咱们都会更有灵感。
t_en: I've got a rough idea... Let's go to Scalegorge Waterscape then. Perhaps we'll be able to find our inspiration beneath the Statue of the High Elder.

t_ch: 您看看，在下光是看着这雄伟的龙尊造像，灵感就跟瀑布似的，刷啦啦地涌进了脑子啊！
t_en: Drink it in. Just looking at this magnificent Statue of the High Elder causes inspiration to flood my brain like a waterfall!

t_ch: 那你开始编吧。
t_en: Then why don't you tell me a story, storyteller?

t_ch: 俗称脑子进水。
t_en: I think they call that going "soft in the head."

t_ch: 这进的哪里是水啊，这是评书话本的大河、口传文学的湍流！
t_en: I wasn't being literal. I mean torrents of storytelling swirling into a waterfall of literature!

t_ch: 「话说，这{NICKNAME}服下『九转升天丹』后，果然功力大涨。可绝灭大君乃是烬灭令使，岂是区区凡人所能及？」
t_en: "It's said that {NICKNAME}'s powers surged after taking the Elixir of Dragon Oil. But alas, the Lord Ravager was still an Emanator of the Ruin Author. How could a mere mortal hope to contend?"

t_ch: 「此时{NICKNAME}想到，持明族乃是龙祖后裔，持明龙尊自然也身怀星神伟力…若能借到龙尊之力，必能使自己神功大成。」
t_en: "It was then that {NICKNAME} realized the Vidyadhara were descendants of dragons, whose high elders naturally possessed Aeonic powers... And if those powers could be borrowed, transcendence shall be achieved!"

t_ch: 你不是说吃了丹药就行吗？
t_en: Wait a minute, didn't you say taking the elixir would be enough already?

t_ch: 怎么神功又要大成了？
t_en: Didn't I already achieve transcendence by taking that dragon oil elixir?

t_ch: 嗐，这些都是文学上的「夸张」手法！在下就学于曜青著名的高等学府「行风璧海」，乃是科班出身的文人，下笔必有章法，绝不会给您乱写的。
t_en: Hey, ever hear of hyperbole? I studied at the Royal Academy on the Yaoqing and I'm a professional playwright by trade. My writing has structure, and I would never be sloppy.

t_ch: 咳咳…「内有神丹脱胎换骨，外有龙尊之力护体，莫说是个小小的令使，就算烬灭祸祖本人，也未必是{NICKNAME}的对手！」
t_en: Ahem... "With the miracle elixir flowing internally and the high elder's power protecting externally, a mere Emanator — or even the Ruin Author themselves — would prove no match for {NICKNAME}!"

t_ch: 又在乱写！
t_en: You're getting sloppy!

t_ch: 又在「夸张」！
t_en: More so-called hyperbole!

t_ch: 也不知纳努克同不同意…
t_en: I'm not so sure Nanook would agree with you...

t_ch: 细节您不要太在意！都说了，这是初稿，之后还要润色改稿的。您主要看故事内容就好。
t_en: Forget about the details! I told you, this is just the first draft, and we'll revise it later. Just focus on the main plot.

t_ch: 嗯…回归正题，这段故事，应当从您在龙尊造像寻找线索开始讲起。
t_en: Hmm... Back to the topic at hand, please. This scene should begin with you searching for clues around the Statue of the High Elder.

t_ch: 在整个故事中加上这段冒险，肯定能把这个「主角模板」充实起来。
t_en: Dropping adventure into every scene of the plot will definitely help us flesh out this Protagonist Model.

t_ch: 估计咱俩又要像上次一样，折腾大半天。所以您还是去稍作一下准备吧，吃点东西垫垫肚子，或者上个洗手间之类的。
t_en: I reckon this'll take the better half of a day just like last time. So why don't you go off and get ready? Fortify that stomach of yours or go to the bathroom first.

t_ch: 等您准备好了，就来这里找我吧。
t_en: Come find me when you're ready.

t_ch: 怎么样，您准备好了吗？
t_en: How's it going? Ready to start?

t_ch: 开始吧。
t_en: Let's begin.

t_ch: 再等我一下。
t_en: Hang on a minute.

t_ch: 好的，您慢慢准备，在下随时可以开始。
t_en: Alright, take your time. I'm ready when you are.

t_ch: 你闯入鳞渊境，就是要向我讨要龙尊的力量吗？可笑，这等东西岂是外族人所能讨要的吗？
t_en: Did you break into Scalegorge Waterscape to try and beg me for the power of the high elder? You're joking! Is this some kind of outworlder joke?

t_ch: 谁在说话？
t_en: Who's speaking?

t_ch: 你躲在哪里了？
t_en: Where are you hiding?

t_ch: 抬头看看罢！我就在你身前！毋要视而不见！
t_en: Up here! I'm right in front of you! Don't turn a blind eye to me!

t_ch: 你是尊雕像？
t_en: Are you a statue?

t_ch: 我乃龙尊雨别造像，久沐日精月华而禀灵性。
t_en: I am the statue of Yubie the high elder. Steeped in the essence of moonlight, I have taken consciousness.

t_ch: 你身为外族，既不认得我是谁，又怎敢贸然前来求取龙尊之力？呵…这力量绝不能给你！
t_en: You're an outworlder who doesn't even know who I am, yet you dare come here to claim the power of the high elder so rashly? Heh... You'll never get your hands on it!

t_ch: 不给拉倒。
t_en: Fine, I don't even want it.

t_ch: 这么容易放弃吗？无名客难道不应该是一群坚韧不拔的旅者吗？
t_en: Quitting so soon? I thought the Nameless were supposed to be tenacious and indomitable?

t_ch: 不能通融一下吗？
t_en: Can't you bend the rules this once, pleaaase~

t_ch: 这是要是能靠「通融一下」就解决的问题，持明族也不至于落到如今这般田地了。
t_en: If this was something that could just be "bent," then the Vidyadhara wouldn't find ourselves in our current predicament.

t_ch: 雕像会说话诶！
t_en: A statue — that can talk!

t_ch: 行了！我本以为你身为无名客，多少也见过些世面，结果竟如此大惊小怪！
t_en: Fine! I foolishly believed that you, as Nameless, would be a well-traveled connoisseur but it seemed you were rather finicky, shall we say!

t_ch: 唉…我也不过是一座雕像罢了，也没什么资格对你说教。
t_en: Sigh... But then again I'm nothing more than a statue. I can hardly lecture you.

t_ch: 这样吧…只要你能通过我的试炼，获得我的承认。我就赐予你当下需要的东西。
t_en: What about... I set a trial for you. Pass it, and you'll gain my recognition. I'll then grant you whatever your wish is.

t_ch: 怎么又是试炼？
t_en: Another trial?

t_ch: 若没有试炼，我该怎么知道你是否值得我帮助呢？
t_en: Without a trial, how would I know whether you were worthy of my help?

t_ch: 我要发大财！
t_en: I wish to be rich!

t_ch: 呵…现在还不是许愿的时候。等你完成了试炼，我们再来讨论这个话题吧。
t_en: Heh... Now's not the time for making wishes. We can talk about this again once you complete the trial.

t_ch: 我的愿望是再要三个愿望！
t_en: My wish is for three more wishes!

t_ch: 我可不是什么万能的许愿机。等你完成了试炼，我们再来讨论这个话题吧。
t_en: I'm not some genie in a lamp. We can talk about this again once you complete the trial.

t_ch: 数百年前，龙尊丹枫误入歧途，掀起「饮月之乱」，在鳞渊境中留下了无数满怀遗恨的蜃影。
t_en: Centuries ago, Dan Feng the high elder got led astray in life, triggering the Sedition of Imbibitor Lunae and leaving countless Mirage Echoes filled with hatred throughout Scalegorge Waterscape.

t_ch: 其中有那么几个蜃影执着于歹毒的恨意，屡衅事端，让人们不堪其扰。请你解决掉那些满怀恶意的蜃影，正好当做是对你的试炼了。
t_en: A few of them still cling to vicious hatred, causing all kinds of ruckus that's really starting to get to people. Please get rid of these ill-willed Mirage Echoes — that's the trial that I have for you.

t_ch: 出发吧。那蜃影积怨不小，流毒无穷。切记小心行事。
t_en: Go forth. They have their grievances, and their vileness knows no end. Tread carefully.

t_ch: 应该算是解决了吧？回去和那个雕像报告吧。
t_en: That should be it, right? Time to go back and report in to that statue.

t_ch: 鳞渊境的怨气散去了一些…想来你已完成了试炼。无名客果然名不虚传……
t_en: The grievances hanging over Scalegorge Waterscape have cleared a little... I think you have indeed completed the trial. You really do live up to the moniker of the Nameless...

t_ch: 既然你顺利完成了第一项试炼，那么接下来，就该是第二项试炼了。
t_en: Now that you've completed the first trial, now it's time for the second trial.

t_ch: 到底还有多少试炼？
t_en: Just exactly how many trials are there now!?

t_ch: 你这是要给我十三试炼呢？
t_en: You're making me do all the Thirteen Labors, aren't you?

t_ch: 别担心，这便是最后一场试炼了。
t_en: Don't worry. This will be your final trial.

t_ch: 丹枫被囚前，将自己所拥至宝藏入鳞渊境的深处，并留下遗言——
t_en: Before his imprisonment, Dan Feng buried all of his treasure deep within Scalegorge Waterscape. They say that before he set out, he spoke these words—

t_ch: 敬请购买《冒险鼹鼠队》！
t_en: "Rules are made to be broken!"

t_ch: 想要龙尊秘宝吗？想要的话…
t_en: "I wish not for dominion, yet I cannot watch the common folks suffer!"

t_ch: 给我适可而止一点……
t_en: What's that about!?

t_ch: 总之，第二场试炼便是希望你能寻回至宝，令其物归原主，重定天命。
t_en: Anyway, the second trial is to find the treasure, return it to its original owner, and reset the wheels of fate.

t_ch: 我只知道那些宝物大致的方位，却不知道它们的具体位置。
t_en: I only know the general vicinity of the treasure but not the exact location of everything.

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}一到这里，就感受到龙祖的气息在空中弥散…{M#他}{F#她}心中暗想：这大概就是龙尊秘宝的所在地吧！」
t_en: "When {NICKNAME} arrived at this place, {M#he}{F#she} immediately smelled the presence of Long in the air and thought: This must be where the secret treasures of the high elder lies!"

t_ch: 真的假的…我鼻子这么灵？让我瞧瞧，这附近有什么能藏宝贝的地方。
t_en: Really? Is my sense of smell that good? Let me see if there's any place to hide a treasure.

t_ch: 这应该就是丹枫留下的宝藏了。拿去找雕像交差吧。
t_en: This looks like one of Dan Feng's treasure items. Report back with it to the statue.

t_ch: 行了，行了，你别砸了，停！停一下！
t_en: Fine, fine. Don't break it, stop! Please stop!

t_ch: 你怎么来了？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 我再不出来，鳞渊境就让你拆成白地了！
t_en: Had I stay put a bit longer, Scalegorge Waterscape would have been razed to the ground by you!

t_ch: 别捣乱，我忙着寻宝呢。
t_en: Hey! Don't interupt! I'm trailblazin' here!

t_ch: 你这是寻宝？你这是破坏古迹！
t_en: You call that trailblazing? You're trail destroying!

t_ch: 是我看走眼了，这古老的龙宫禁不起你这么折腾。
t_en: I made an error in judgment. This old Dragon Palace cannot withstand this kind of abuse.

t_ch: 我认可你的{RUBY_B#破坏力}赤诚之心{RUBY_E#}了，这个测试就跳过吧，跳过。
t_en: Lemme just acknowledge your {RUBY_B#destructive power}Heartfelt Devotion{RUBY_E#}. And now let's just skip this part of the trial.

t_ch: 回龙尊造像那里找我吧…咱们直接进行下一步就好。
t_en: Come back to the Statue of the High Elder and find me... We can go straight to the next step.

t_ch: 我果然没有看错你。能完成这些试炼，说明你是一个值得信赖的人。
t_en: So I was right about you. Being able to complete these trials proves you a trustworthy person.

t_ch: 真的吗？要不再试几次？
t_en: Really? Sure you don't want a couple more trials?

t_ch: 不必了，不必了。
t_en: No, thanks. We're good.

t_ch: 你很有眼光！
t_en: Well said! You sure have good taste, I'll give you that!

t_ch: 道之为物，其变有三。既然通过了前两项试炼，那我就将最后、也是最重要的考验托付给你——先别急着说我不讲信用……
t_en: The truth always comes in threes. You may have passed the first two trials, but now comes the final and most important test — and please don't tell me that I'm not trusting...

t_ch: 因为这第三项考验便是你所寻求的答案，龙尊之力。
t_en: Because this third trial is the answer which you seek — the power of the high elder.

t_ch: 我持明族轮回相续，循环自在，上一任龙尊逊位蜕生，新任龙尊就要重新降世了！
t_en: We Vidyadhara naturally reincarnate in cyclical continuity. As the previous high elder has abdicated and undergone hatching rebirth, a new high elder will be reborn once more!

t_ch: 我要你找到这颗持明卵，为新任龙尊接生。此后，这新任龙尊将成为你的助力，帮助你渡过眼下的难关。
t_en: I want you to find this Vidyadhara egg and deliver the rebirth of the new high elder. From then on, this new high elder will be your help, and support you in overcoming any hardships you may encounter.

t_ch: 去吧，无名客，你索求的助力便在眼前！
t_en: Go, Nameless. The help you seek is at hand!

t_ch: 我已为你指明了方向，去吧，无名客！你索求的助力便在眼前！
t_en: I've pointed out the way for you. Go, Nameless! The help you seek is at hand!

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}找到了那颗流光溢彩的持明卵，当中的确正有个生灵要破茧而出。{NICKNAME}小心翼翼地开始为新任龙尊接生。」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} found the luminescent Vidyadhara egg, and indeed there was a creature about to break free from incubation. And so, {NICKNAME} carefully delivered the new high elder into re-existence."

t_ch: 你管这叫接生？
t_en: You call that delivering a newborn?

t_ch: 这不是修理东西的界面吗？
t_en: Isn't that the screen for fixing stuff?

t_ch: 这是咱们仙舟惯用的文学表现手法啊，没什么特别奇怪的吧？
t_en: I'm just using a typical Xianzhou literary device. Nothing special about it, m'kay?

t_ch: 开锁是兆算玉璃…
t_en: It's locked by Abacus Circuitry...

t_ch: 修理是兆算玉璃…
t_en: It's fixed by Abacus Circuitry...

t_ch: 操作机械是兆算玉璃…
t_en: Its operating system is running on Abacus Circuitry...

t_ch: 接生怎么还是兆算玉璃！
t_en: Remind me again, why does the delivery also involve Abacus Circuitry!?

t_ch: 呃…在下承认，接生这么搞是有点牵强了！但我一时也没想到还能用什么表现手法啊。
t_en: Sigh... I admit, it's a bit far-fetched! But I couldn't think of any other way to express things.

t_ch: 唉，算了！初稿先这样吧！具体怎么描写，就交给未来的西衍先生去头疼吧！
t_en: Ah, forget it! That's our first draft for now! As for the exact details, I'll leave that up to Future Mr. Xiyan to worry about!

t_ch: 总而言之…「在{NICKNAME}的帮助下，新生的龙尊自持明卵中顺利地蜕生了。」
t_en: All in all... "With the help of {NICKNAME}, the newborn high elder of the Vidyadhara successfully hatched from their egg and was thus reborn."

t_ch: 我的好大儿啊！
t_en: Wow, its size is huge!

t_ch: 哎，矜持一点！
t_en: Hey, stay classy!

t_ch: 一出生就这么高？
t_en: Pretty tall for a newborn, huh?

t_ch: 龙尊嘛，自然与凡人不同，那是见风就长！
t_en: The high elder is naturally superior to the ordinary and grows immediately upon the touch of the wind!

t_ch: 刚出生就穿着衣服？
t_en: Born wearing clothes!?

t_ch: 这有什么奇怪的？仙舟还有话本小说里写一公子哥刚出生嘴里就含着一块玉兆，能算前生后世五百年呢！
t_en: What's so weird about that? There's another Xianzhou classic tale that tells of a rich boy born with a silver spoon — or rather, jade abacus — in its mouth. The newborn could make divinations five hundred years back and into the future!

t_ch: 「新生的龙尊怔怔地看着{NICKNAME}…此时，{NICKNAME}应当负起接生者的责任，为龙尊命名，此之谓——龙得其名而有灵！」
t_en: "The newborn high elder stared blankly at {NICKNAME}... At this moment, {NICKNAME} should bear the sacred responsibility of scripting a name for this newborn high elder. Dragons derive power from their names!"

t_ch: 为什么是我来起名…
t_en: Why do I have to name it...

t_ch: 因为您是这个故事的主角啊！「{NICKNAME}将新生的龙尊命名为——」
t_en: Because you're the protagonist of this story! "{NICKNAME} named the new high elder as —"

t_ch: 丹恒
t_en: Dan Heng

t_ch: 丹心如恒，好名字！
t_en: Dan Heng, the Everlasting — a great name!

t_ch: 冷面小青龙
t_en: Cold Dragon Young

t_ch: 面寒如霜，身动如龙，好名字！
t_en: Cold as frost, but nimble as a dragon — a fitting name!

t_ch: 龙尊MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak>型
t_en: High Elder MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak>

t_ch: 好一个MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak>型，人如其名一望即知有多强！好名字！
t_en: What a great MK<unbreak>2000</unbreak> model — just as its name suggests, it's an upgrade. A splendid name!

t_ch: 赵大宝
t_en: John Doe

t_ch: ……咳咳！定是我才疏学浅，未能识得这姓字真意。但龙尊既钟于造化灵气，那必然是块大宝贝！好名字！
t_en: ...Ummm.... I fail to see how that makes sense. But since you are the one who picked this, it must be a great name!

t_ch: 「一番际遇——{TEXTJOIN#59}！持明族的新任首领，由此横空出世！」
t_en: "As fate would dictate — {TEXTJOIN#59}! The new leader of the Vidyadhara has been reborn!"

t_ch: 「可正当两人沉浸于新生的喜悦中时，危险却悄然靠近。一群药王秘传找到了这里。」
t_en: "But just as the two were immersed in the joys of life reborn, crisis loomed imminent. A horde of Disciples of Sanctus Medicus had found this location."

t_ch: 刚刚蜕生的龙尊，还有那个干掉了我们很多兄弟姐妹化外民！你们果然在这里！
t_en: The freshly-reborn high elder and that outworlder who took out so many of our brothers and sisters! So this is where you are!

t_ch: 快，趁他们现在势单力薄，做掉他们！上！
t_en: Quick, take advantage of their weak position to rid them once and for all. Go!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，不必担心。我会以云吟秘法护你周全…这群宵小之徒根本不足为惧！
t_en: Fear not, {NICKNAME}. Let me chant a cloudhymn to protect you... These schmucks are nothing to worry about!

t_ch: 「面对来势汹汹的敌人，{NICKNAME}气运丹田，大喝一声——」
t_en: "Faced with the advancing enemy, {NICKNAME} concentrated all {F#her}{M#his} qi to {F#her}{M#his} Core Radix, as {F#she}{M#he} shouted —"

t_ch: 你要战，便来战！
t_en: If fight is what you want, then come and take it!

t_ch: 端的是声若惊雷气如虹，{NICKNAME}一声怒叱惊破敌胆！大战就此展开——
t_en: On the other hand, the sound was like a clap of thunder as {NICKNAME} let out an angry roar, scaring the heck out of all the enemies! And with that, the battle had begun—

t_ch: 谢谢你，{TEXTJOIN#59}！
t_en: Thank you, {TEXTJOIN#59}!

t_ch: 虽然出生不足一炷香，但龙尊却不愧是持明尊长，回了一声「不客气」，大战就此展开——
t_en: Despite being mere minutes old, the new high elder was nevertheless the high elder of the Vidyadhara, and responded, "You're welcome" as the battle began—

t_ch: 我什么也做不到啊！
t_en: This is above my pay grade!

t_ch: 您莫不是在对什么暗号？眼下哪是说这种丧气话的时候！
t_en: Could it be some kind of passphrase? Now is hardly the time to say such despondent words!

t_ch: 得了，但见龙尊一声清叱，大战就此展开——
t_en: Alright! But the high elder gave a clear shout as the battle began—

t_ch: 嘤嘤嘤！
t_en: *sob in tears*

t_ch: 你哭什么？啊，是了！定是{NICKNAME}初为{F#人母}{M#人父}，虽非亲生亲养，却也感受到了天地禀赋于人的那份亲情感动！
t_en: What are you crying about? Ah, yes! {NICKNAME} has become a {F#mother}{M#father} for the very first time and despite not being one by the biological definition, our hero still feels that heavenly gift of affection!

t_ch: 但此处已容不得感情戏展开了，但见龙尊一声清叱，大战就此展开——
t_en: But there's no room for any emotional drama to play out here as the high elder gave a clear shout, and the battle began—

t_ch: 这都什么破剧情！
t_en: What kind of hack plot is this!?

t_ch: 是，是，这剧情写的确实不咋样。但你就说剧情推进得爽不爽吧？
t_en: Sure, okay fine. The plot is bad. But the plot is progressing along nicely, isn't it?

t_ch: 但见龙尊一声清叱，大战就此展开——
t_en: But the high elder gave a clear shout as the battle began—

t_ch: 「此战之后，{NICKNAME}与{TEXTJOIN#59}便成为歃血同盟，任谁也无法离间！」
t_en: "After this battle, {NICKNAME} and {TEXTJOIN#59} forged an alliance in blood between which none could ever hope to drive a wedge!"

t_ch: 「列位稍安勿躁，下一场，便是列位看官期待已久的武打戏——『俏行者慈心护襁褓，小龙尊一怒动海潮』！」
t_en: "Be patient, everyone. The next act, dear listeners, is our long-awaited martial arts fight scene: 'Traveler Treasures Heart of Scions, Dragonlord Commands Wrath of Seas!'"

t_ch: 哎呀，这样一来，这折子戏也算是完成了……
t_en: Ah, if we put it like that, then this act will be complete too...

t_ch: 「期待已久的武打戏」去哪了？
t_en: Where did the "long-awaited martial arts scene" end up going?

t_ch: 之前不是都和您说了吗，武打戏是最好写的。只消在您的「主角模板」上再添几笔就行啦！
t_en: Didn't I mention? Martial arts scenes are the easiest of all to write. Just a couple of strokes on your Protagonist Model is all it takes!

t_ch: 西衍先生像之前那样开始奋笔疾书。
t_en: Mr. Xiyan began scribbling at a furious pace, as before.

t_ch: 不一会儿，他便颇为开心似的长叹了一声——这似乎是某种西衍先生写作结束时的习惯性动作——然后便将「主角模板」交还给了你。
t_en: He soon let out a long, satisfied sigh, which seemed to be a habitual pattern for Mr. Xiyan's writing process, and handed the Protagonist Model over to you.

t_ch: 这一折戏也算是完成了…您抽空看看吧，有什么意见尽管提。
t_en: This scene is complete... Take some time to have a look at it, and feel free to share any feedback with me.

t_ch: 接下来应该是您这段故事的高潮和尾声了，在下可得好好想想该怎么写。
t_en: What follows should be the climax and the end of your story, so I need to think carefully about how it's written.

t_ch: 今天时候也不早了，咱俩都辛苦了。在下就先回星槎海了，有什么灵感下次再交流吧。
t_en: It's already getting late, and we've both put in a lot of effort. I will head back to Starskiff Haven and let's keep in touch if any inspiration strikes.

t_ch: 回见了您呐～
t_en: So long~

t_ch: 有什么新故事吗？
t_en: Any new stories?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}{M#先生}{F#小姐}，您好哇！果然是放不下在下的新奇故事吧？谁能拒绝成为一个爽快故事的主角呢？
t_en: {M#Mister}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, hello! You can't let go of my spellbinding story, can you? Who can resist being the protagonist of their own epic adventure?

t_ch: 哈哈，「主角模板」真是妙用无穷，我又编了些新故事，正等着您洗耳恭听呢！
t_en: Haha, the Protagonist Model is so incredibly useful that I've ended up writing a few more stories. I wonder what you think of them?

t_ch: 我好恨呐…
t_en: I hate it...

t_ch: 我已为地衡司效力五百年，眼看就能获得资格在闲云天购置宅邸了！
t_en: I've worked for the Realm-Keeping Commission for five hundred years, and I'll soon qualify to buy a townhouse in the Drifters Sanctum!

t_ch: 可惜啊！经此大难之后，我的转世之身又要重新干起了……
t_en: What a pity! After this disaster, my reincarnated body has to keep working...

t_ch: 看来不是这个……
t_en: This doesn't seem to be the one...

t_ch: 存在感…
t_en: Sense of existence...

t_ch: 他们总嘲笑我没什么存在感…我该如何得到龙师长老们的重视呢？
t_en: They always mock me for being nothing... How can I earn the respect of the Preceptors?

t_ch: 为何每次一有麻烦的任务时，我的存在感就出现了呢？
t_en: Why does my existence become visible every time a troublesome task presents itself?

t_ch: 看来不是这个……
t_en: This doesn't seem to be the one...

t_ch: 文档目录第三千六百三…
t_en: Document directory article <unbreak>3630</unbreak>...

t_ch: 潜渊阁文档目录第三千六百三十二条……
t_en: Office of Deep Sources document directory article <unbreak>3632</unbreak>...

t_ch: 龙尊梦兆录？不，不是这本。偌大的持明族图书馆竟然没有实现自动化管理！可恶……
t_en: The high elder's dream divinations? No, not this one. How have the Vidyadhara not figured out an automatic file management system yet for their massive library!? Tsk...

t_ch: 看来不是这个……
t_en: This doesn't seem to be the one...

t_ch: 陪伴我的小狐狸，离开了…
t_en: My little foxy has run off...

t_ch: 我在身为持明卵时曾梦见一只小小的狐狸在我身旁徘徊嬉水。
t_en: Back when I was just an egg, I dreamed there was a little fox playing around in the water with me.

t_ch: 当我长大了，在人海中找到了她，她却即将寿终正寝…一念及此，我就忍不住泪如雨下。
t_en: When I grew up, I found her out there in the world, just as she was about to die of old age... Just the thought of that made me burst into tears.

t_ch: 看来不是这个……
t_en: This doesn't seem to be the one...

t_ch: 我本该被选为龙尊…
t_en: They should've picked me as the high elder...

t_ch: 我本该受遴选成为龙尊…可恨，可恨的丹枫啊！是他横夺了我应有的权力！
t_en: I was supposed to be chosen as high elder... That cursed, cursed Dan Feng! It was he who stole my rightful power from me!

t_ch: 结果如何？龙师们所托非人，持明族陷于浩劫！哈哈哈哈，这都是他们应得的果报！
t_en: How did things end up? The Preceptors got the wrong person and plunged the Vidyadhara into crisis! Haha, that was exactly what they deserved!

t_ch: 看来就是这个了！
t_en: Looks like this is it!

t_ch: 你身上…有那可恶的龙尊的气息…可恶啊啊啊啊！我好恨啊啊啊啊！
t_en: You... You have the stink of that cursed high elder about you... How terrible! I hate it so much!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}顺利完成试炼，回到了龙尊造像前。
t_en: {NICKNAME} successfully completed the trial and returned to the Statue of the High Elder.

t_ch: 按照龙尊造像的指引，{NICKNAME}找到了龙尊大人的藏宝之处。
t_en: {NICKNAME} found the high elder's secret treasure trove thanks to the statue's guidance.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}顺利完成试炼，回到了龙尊造像前。
t_en: {NICKNAME} successfully completed the trial and returned to the Statue of the High Elder.

t_ch: 按照龙尊造像的指引，{NICKNAME}找到了即将降生于世的新任龙尊。
t_en: {NICKNAME} found the soon-to-be-born impending high elder thanks to the statue's guidance.

t_ch: 咱们的评书什么时候完稿？
t_en: When are we going to be done with our storytelling?

t_ch: 嗐…净顾着说评书了，差点忘了这事。这个事情在下早有准备，您就瞧好儿吧！
t_en: Hmm... I was so busy with all this storytelling that I almost forgot. I've made preparations for this, just you wait and see!

t_ch: 下一折——「镇玄根义侠斗幻胧，斩妖邪孤勇建奇功」，在下已经构思得差不多了，就等您来做最后确认了。
t_en: Our next scene: "Warrior Quells Phantylia, Hero Earns Regalia." I've already put this whole scene together, but I'm just waiting for your final confirmation.

t_ch: 走吧，咱们直接去鳞渊境，在下给您准备了一个小惊喜。
t_en: Let's go, straight to Scalegorge Waterscape. I've prepared a little surprise for you.

t_ch: 我有不祥的预感…
t_en: I've got a bad feeling about this...

t_ch: 翻译翻译什么叫惊喜。
t_en: Define "surprise." Explain to me like I'm 5.

t_ch: 哎呀，现在说破了就没意思了。马上您就能见到了。
t_en: It'd spoil everything if I just told you now. You'll see soon enough.

t_ch: 您别担心，在下办事有多可靠，大家有目共睹。咱们鳞渊境汇合吧！
t_en: Don't worry. Everyone can vouch for my reliability. Catch you at Scalegorge Waterscape!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您可算是来了。给您介绍一下，这位就是我给您准备的「惊喜」。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you've made it. Why don't you meet this "surprise" I've got waiting for you?

t_ch: 这不是评书里的角色吗？
t_en: Isn't this the character from before?

t_ch: 嘿嘿，吓一跳吧。
t_en: Heehee, freaked you out, right?

t_ch: 拜见龙尊大人！
t_en: Salutations, Your Highness!

t_ch: 不许跪。咱仙舟上早不兴这套礼节了。
t_en: Kneel ye not. This custom has long disappeared from Xianzhou etiquette.

t_ch: 您别害怕，这位是米糕没幻戏社的著名演员石韵老师。
t_en: Don't be scared! This is Shiyun, a famed actor from the Mellow-Golden-Mayor Immersia Club.

t_ch: 小衍啊，别叫我真名，影响我入戏。用角色的名字称呼我吧，就那个…什么来着？{TEXTJOIN#59}，对，叫我{TEXTJOIN#59}就好。
t_en: Ssh, don't mention my real name around here! You'll kill my flow and take me out of the zone. Call me by my role, er... Which is what, again? {TEXTJOIN#59}, that's right. Call me {TEXTJOIN#59}.

t_ch: 好的{TEXTJOIN#59}老师，您所言极是。{NICKNAME}，人家米糕没相中了咱们写的这个评书，想买下幻戏改编版权，所以让{TEXTJOIN#59}老师莅临指导。
t_en: Alright then, {TEXTJOIN#59}. You are emphatically correct. {NICKNAME}, the Mellow-Golden-Mayor Immersia Club picked up our story and is interested in potentially buying the adaptation rights. So let's show {TEXTJOIN#59} a very warm welcome, okay?

t_ch: 您看看，他这一化上妆，和咱们想象中的{TEXTJOIN#59}真是一模一样啊！
t_en: Look, he's made up exactly like I imagined {TEXTJOIN#59} would be!

t_ch: 这是位童星？
t_en: Is this a child actor?

t_ch: 童星？哈哈哈哈，我刚出道时确是小鲜肉，但如今已是从业五百多年的老戏骨了！
t_en: Child actor? Haha, I may have been fresh meat when I first debuted, but that was over five hundred years ago. I'm an old hand now!

t_ch: 行了，闲话少叙，小衍，讲讲你们的这个评书吧。我评估一下这角色值不值得我出演。
t_en: Okay, enough gossip for now. Xiyan, tell me more of your storytelling. Let's see if this role is worth my taking on.

t_ch: 得～嘞～「话说，这{NICKNAME}如今吞服了神丹脱胎换骨，身边更是跟着持明龙尊，一时可谓是风头无两，所向披靡！」
t_en: O-kay~ "{NICKNAME} has been born anew after swallowing the miracle elixir, and has teamed up with the high elder of the Vidyadhara. Right now, our hero is the talk of the town, unmatched in power and might!"

t_ch: 「当此之时，正乃罗浮仙舟危急存亡之秋，绝灭大君幻胧率烬灭匪兵杀到，誓要将这罗浮搅个天翻地覆！」
t_en: "This was a time when Xianzhou Luofu fell into crisis, with the Lord Ravager Phantylia leading the forces of the Ruin Author on a rampage as they vowed to destroy the Luofu!"

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}横刀立马，挡在幻胧身前，大喝一声：『妖孽，今时此处，便是你埋骨之时、葬身之处！』」
t_en: "{NICKNAME}, with weapon in hand, stood before Phantylia, bellowing out: 'Beast! Right here, right now: This will be your final resting place!'"

t_ch: 「{TEXTJOIN#59}暗暗驱动云吟奇术，护在{NICKNAME}身边，大战一触即发，罗浮仙舟的命运系于此战！」
t_en: "{TEXTJOIN#59} secretly activated the cloudhymn to protect {NICKNAME}. A battle could break out at any moment, and the fate of the Xianzhou Luofu rested on the outcome of this fight!"

t_ch: 「随着绝灭大君轰然倒下，这最后一折书，便到此结束了……」
t_en: "As the Lord Ravager met its demise, the curtain fell on this final scene in the story..."

t_ch: 幻胧不长这样啊？
t_en: I don't think Phantylia really looks like that.

t_ch: 啊？是吗？在下可没机缘见幻胧，也不想见。
t_en: Oh, really? I've never had the chance to personally see Phantylia and I hope I never do.

t_ch: 您既然亲眼见过幻胧，能给在下讲讲它长什么样子吗？
t_en: Since you've seen Phantylia before, could you tell me a bit more about what it looks like?

t_ch: 背后似乎长着植物…
t_en: There seemed to be plants growing on its back...

t_ch: 嗯…大概明白了。还有什么特征吗？
t_en: Mmm... I think I got it. And any other features?

t_ch: 身形非常巨大…
t_en: It's humongous... monstrously huge...

t_ch: 身形巨大…我心里稍微有数了。
t_en: Monstrously huge... I'm sure I can imagine.

t_ch: 看起来特别凶恶。
t_en: It looks incredibly ferocious.

t_ch: 哦哦哦！您这么一说在下大概就明白了。行的，这个等之后改稿的时候肯定会修正的。
t_en: Ooh, ooh! When you put it like that, I think I've got it. Okay, this is definitely getting updated when we do our revisions.

t_ch: 幻胧长什么样我不是很关心，我只有一个问题…这就完了？
t_en: I don't care much for what Phantylia looks like. My only question is... Is that it?

t_ch: 呃…还有一点尾声、后日谈什么的，暂时还没想好怎么写，但主线部分确实就到此结束了。
t_en: Er... There's still a coda, an epilogue that comes afterward. I haven't figured out exactly how it goes, but the main plot indeed reaches its conclusion here.

t_ch: 我觉得吧，这个故事还缺乏一点更精彩的冒险。作为一个从业多年的老戏骨，我个人有一些建议。
t_en: I feel like this story is lacking for scintillating adventure. As an old theater hound, I've got a few pointers if you'd like to hear.

t_ch: 请您不吝赐教！
t_en: If you could please enlighten us!

t_ch: 第一、{NICKNAME}缺乏一些更吸引人的成长。吃个丹药就变强，这种东西可能在你们评书界行得通，但我们幻戏业界早就不流行这种过时的仙侠幻戏了。
t_en: Firstly, {NICKNAME}'s character development needs to be more compelling. Swallowing a medicinal pellet to get stronger may work in your world of storytelling, but it's long gone out of style in the Immersia genre.

t_ch: 第二、找到龙尊后直接开始最终决战，这显得龙尊这个角色很工具人。应该更丰满他在剧情中的作用。
t_en: Secondly, rolling straight into the decisive battle after finding the high elder makes that character seem like a plot device. His role should be more fleshed out.

t_ch: 第三、你得给我加戏。你评书说成什么样跟我没关系，但既然要改编成幻戏，那就得多给我点戏份。米糕没可就是为了这个，才准备买改编版权的。
t_en: Thirdly, give me some more screen time. I don't care in the slightest how your story gets told, but if it's getting adapted into an Immersia, then you need to give me more screen time. That's what the Mellow-Golden-Mayor Immersia Club wants to see and why they're interested in this acquisition.

t_ch: 想加戏你早说啊…
t_en: Why didn't you say so earlier...

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小弟弟}，看来你对咱们幻戏业界的作风很熟悉啊。
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Dude}, seems like you know your way around the Immersia industry.

t_ch: 戏霸…！
t_en: What a diva...!

t_ch: {F#小姑娘}{M#小弟弟}说话真不客气啊。哎，还真让你说对了。
t_en: {F#Lil' lady}{M#Lil' dude}, that's not very polite, is it? But hey, you're not wrong.

t_ch: 要不你来当这主角？
t_en: With an attitude like that, why don't you take my part and play the protagonist instead?

t_ch: 什么话！我一个德艺双馨的老前辈，怎么能横夺短生种的戏份？这种事得加演出费才行！
t_en: Impossible! How could I, a seasoned thespian of esteemed virtue and skill, take a role away from a short-life species such as yourself? Besides, casting me in a leading role will cost you extra!

t_ch: 你们也体谅一下我的苦衷…我都五百多岁了，身体不长个，我就只能一直演些孩童角色啊。
t_en: Please also try to understand what a difficult situation I'm in here... I'm already five hundred years old, but I've stopped growing, so child roles are all I can do.

t_ch: 前几天还有个幻戏剧组要把我皮肤涂成绿色，演个什么指点主角武功的千岁高人…真不是人过的日子，还是演年少的龙尊比较有戏。
t_en: A few days back, an Immersia troupe wanted to paint my whole body green and have me play the role of some wise old master who spoke backwards and tutored a young protagonist in some mystical martial arts... That's gonna be so humiliating. I'd much rather play a young high elder of Vidyadhara without having to wear any prosthetics or paint.

t_ch: 制片人可说了，我能不能焕发事业第二春，全指着你二位这个评书改编的幻戏了。
t_en: My producer even said that whether I can revitalize my career all depends on your adaptation of this story into an Immersia.

t_ch: 您可别说这丧气话，在下刚才稍微构思了一下…您的建议很有道理，您的诉求也很好满足……
t_en: Hey, don't be so demoralizing! I've just come up with some new ideas right now... Your feedback is well-received, and it's easy enough to make adjustments accordingly...

t_ch: 「{NICKNAME}问{TEXTJOIN#59}道：『这绝灭大君，翻手为云覆手为雨，我等肉体凡胎，该如何与之匹敌呢？』」
t_en: "{NICKNAME} asked {TEXTJOIN#59}: 'This Lord Ravager has the very powers of this world at its fingertips. How can we mortals hope to ever defeat it?'"
